free_text: A measurement of the 22-Ne(n,gamma)23-Ne capture cross
section at a stellar temperature of KT = 25 keV
Neutrons were produced near the threshold.
The neutron spectrum was similar to a Maxwellian
spectrum with a temperature of 25 keV.
Activation techniques. The Ne-22 capture cross
section was determined relative to the well-known
Au-197 cross section in two steps. In the first step
the Ne-22 activity was measured relative to five
different neutron capture reactions on Kr. In the
second step the same Kr-reactions were measured
A fast cyclic activation system with a fast sample
changer was used. Both the irradiation and counting
phases were adjusted to be 38 sec.
The activity was counted with a GeLi-detector,
with a relative efficiency of 28 percent and an energy
resolution of 2.4 keV at 1.33 MeV. To obtain a fast
cyclic time, the detector was placed close to the
neutron generation. It was therefore heavily shielded.
During the activity counting phase the neutron
generation was stopped by a proton beam stop in the
The neutron flux was monitored by a Li-6 glass
In the first step, four pressurized gas samples with
different mixtures and weights of neon gas, enriched
to 99.9% in Ne-22, and natural krypton gas were
used. The gas content of the four samples was
the containers were stainless steel spheres, 20 mm in
diameter, 0.5 mm wall thickness. For background
determination, an empty stainless steel sphere was
used. In the second step, the Kr reactions were
measured relative to Au using zeolite tablets, 6 mm
in diameter, loaded with 52.7 cm3 Kr per gram of
sample, sandwiched between two Au foils of the same
diameter or an Au foil between two zeolite tablets.
The Au foils had a weight of 5 - 10 mg. Sixteen
individual runs of the various samples were performed.
